title: Author RED integration tests + AST-only-sanitizer harness + parity-audit + obsolete-test enumeration
implemented: Extended existing AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE thread-local check (added in field_access_info-extension-v1 M1, commit 49ed30c) by 3 LOC at taint.rs:1096 (detect_sanitizer) — mirrors the L816-818 detect_sources pattern exactly. The AstOnlyTestModeGuard now uniformly short-circuits sources, sinks, AND sanitizers.

Authored crates/tldr-core/tests/sanitize_breaks_flow_per_language.rs — 35 tests: 19 sanitize_breaks_flow_<lang>_sanitizer_truncates_flow_via_compute_taint covering all 16 languages + Ruby Rack::Utils.escape_html parity-gap regression guard + PHP intval call form + PHP (int) cast form; plus 16 *_in_string_literal_does_not_sanitize regression guards (closes-#24-shaped FP class extended to sanitizers).

Authored crates/tldr-core/tests/sanitize_breaks_flow_ast_only_harness.rs — 18 ast_only mirror tests: 16 base-language mirrors + Ruby Rack::Utils + PHP cast (the 2 pre-flagged parity-gap targets). 2 more than the contract's 16-mirror baseline; the extra discriminative coverage is harmless overage on the RED gate.

Updated docstring of analyze_ast_only at common/integ_helpers.rs:94 to reflect that the AstOnlyTestModeGuard now also short-circuits sanitizers (post-M1 3-LOC extension at taint.rs:1096). Reused the existing helper — NO new helper added; M2 wiring will exercise the same guard.

Wrote reports/M1-test-enumeration.json — exhaustive list of 24 obsolete tests across taint_tests.rs (20) + tests/security_tests.rs (4) gating M4 atomic deletion. Premortem A2 baseline verified at HEAD; ZERO augmentations needed — all 24 line numbers match.

Wrote reports/M1-parity-audit.json — per-bank diff for the 7 verify-during-M1 langs (TS/JS, Go, Java, Swift, CSharp, Scala, Elixir). 6 langs at full parity; 1 gap surfaced: TS/JS Zod-style `.parse(`/`.safeParse(` regex entry has no AST counterpart. M3 should add a Numeric entry with member_patterns: [("*", "parse"), ("*", "safeParse")] to TYPESCRIPT_AST_SANITIZERS. NOT blocking for M2 RED→GREEN since the existing M1 ast_only fixtures use parseInt() (already AST-covered).
Captured RED state to reports/M1-red-capture.txt (regular path) and reports/M1-ast-only-red-capture.txt (AST-only path).

outcomes.sanitize_breaks_flow_per_language: 21 passed / 14 failed at HEAD post-M1. Failures are the closes-#24-shaped string-literal FP class (regex bank does NOT respect string-literal boundaries) — exactly the 14 *_in_string_literal_does_not_sanitize tests where regex catches the substring INSIDE a string literal and incorrectly marks the variable as sanitized. Documented per-language in reports/M1-red-capture.txt. ALL 19 sanitize_breaks_flow_<lang>_sanitizer_truncates_flow tests PASS at HEAD (regex bank still active — additive coverage proof confirmed).
outcomes.sanitize_breaks_flow_ast_only_harness: 0 passed / 18 failed — ALL FAIL at HEAD pre-M2 (RED gate). detect_sanitizer regex bank short-circuited via AST_ONLY_TEST_MODE; AST sanitizer dispatch is dead code at the worklist call sites. M2 wires build_sanitizer_ast_index → expects 16 of 18 RED→GREEN at M2 (with ruby_rack_utils + php_int_cast also flipping at M2 per premortem A1; the 2 PHP intval/Ruby to_i extras are simple AST-covered cases that flip at M2 too). NET: ALL 18 expected GREEN by M2-end.

string_literal_failures_at_head.rationale: Languages whose regex bank produces a match against text inside a string literal FAIL pre-M2. Cpp/PHP regexes happen to be more constrained (the substring forms used in those test fixtures don't match) — those happen to PASS at HEAD. Post-M4 (regex bank deleted, AST is_in_string filter active in build_sanitizer_ast_index), ALL 16 must PASS.

regression_check.rr_module_function_integ_test: 20/21 PASS — 1 pre-existing carry-forward failure (ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint ast_only) unchanged from FAI-v1 M4/M5 reports; documented as orthogonal bare-`gets` AST shape gap. ZERO M1-induced regressions.

checks.cargo_test_critical_baselines: PASS (val001a/val001b/val002 (3 files)/val003/rr_framework/rr_stdlib/rr_baseline/rr_module_function — all unchanged from HEAD pre-M1)
checks.cargo_clippy: not run for M1 (per stop_thresholds; clippy gate is in M2 via stop_threshold list); workspace compiles cleanly with zero new warnings beyond what HEAD already produces

discoveries: TS/JS Zod-style sanitizer gap: TYPESCRIPT_PATTERNS regex bank includes `.(parse|safeParse)\s*\(` (entry 3 at L534) but TYPESCRIPT_AST_SANITIZERS has only 2 entries covering parseInt/Number/parseFloat (Numeric) and encodeURIComponent/DOMPurify.sanitize (Html). M3 should add a third Numeric entry with member_patterns [("*", "parse"), ("*", "safeParse")]. Per dispatch-contract per_language_verdict TypeScript_JavaScript line 317, this was anticipated as a possible M3 addition. The M1 RED gate is unaffected because the M1 fixtures use parseInt() (AST-covered).

TaintFlow has no `tainted` field: dispatch contract plan.md §4 example uses `result.flows.iter().filter(|f| f.tainted).count()` but TaintFlow at taint.rs:218 does NOT have a `tainted` field. TaintSink does (L210). Switched assertion strategy to `result.sanitized_vars.contains("safe")` — the canonical signal that sanitizer dispatch fired, independent of per-language flow-construction precision (some languages detect sources/sinks but don't materialize a TaintFlow when an intermediate var is involved).

common/mod.rs convention mismatch: dispatch contract instructions said `#[path = "common/mod.rs"] mod common;` but the actual repo uses `#[path = "common/integ_helpers.rs"] mod common;` (no mod.rs file in tests/common/). Followed the actual repo convention.

analyze helper SSA mismatch: common::analyze (SSA-on) produces zero flows for several languages (Java/Swift/CSharp/etc.) where the rr_baseline tests use SSA-off and successfully detect sources/sinks. Created a private analyze() helper in sanitize_breaks_flow_per_language.rs that calls analyze_with_ssa(..., false) to match the rr_baseline convention. analyze_ast_only is unchanged because it already runs SSA-off per its existing implementation.

loc_delta_actual.taint.rs: +8 (3 LOC logic + 5 LOC comment block matching existing L811-818 / L928-935 style)
loc_delta_actual.common/integ_helpers.rs: +10 (docstring update for analyze_ast_only — clarifies that the guard now covers sanitizers post-M1)
loc_delta_actual.delta_explanation: Contract estimated +203 (M1 = 3-LOC extension + ~200 LOC harness). Actual is much higher because each test fixture has both a sanitized variant + an unsafe variant + assertions across 35 + 18 = 53 tests. Per-test docstrings + multi-line raw fixtures + dispatch-contract-mandated mixed-case names produce more LOC. The 35 sanitize_breaks_flow tests were anticipated as 'pairs' in the original budget; the actual is 19 sanitize_breaks (each pair) + 16 string-literal guards. Conservative LOC estimate proven low.

next_milestone.title: Wire detect_sanitizer_ast — extend process_block + SsaPropagateCtx + add WALK-ONCE-INDEX-BY-LINE helper
next_milestone.expected_red_to_green: 16-18 of 18 sanitize_breaks_flow_ast_only tests transition RED→GREEN at M2 (per premortem A1, both pre-flagged parity gaps already at HEAD; the 2 extras Ruby to_i + PHP intval flip too).
